converters.name: Chidubem Ojukwu
converters.experience: This was more thrilling and rewarding than anything I had done before!

converters.worstTask: Stage 1: Nigerian Startup Brand Audit & Analysis
converters.takeaway: I got to learn a lot about value proposition from MenuCard. TechiesMatch taught me to pay attention to what really matters when setting up communities and websites (something being presentable does not mean it's still solid), and FlareTag taught me how to work well with a team, and how to build up a product from the ground up in terms of marketing.

converters.grateful: BK, Awesome Grace Adenike, Ana Inyang, Menim, BintYahya, Osajimere, Nwa, MiKEY, Chief ZiKKY, G_, Confidence

converters.next: I'd focus more on SEO Optimisation and then rest. I need rest.
converters.advice: Sales and Marketing is not easy. Don't ever think it is. It could be an enjoyable ride too, so brace up!

converters.name: Biira Bridget
converters.experience:
My experience was very good and truly awesome. I finished the last cohort as a Product Manager finalist, and as a practicing product manager in real organizations, I’ve seen firsthand how product managers wear many hats. To succeed in that role, I knew I needed to expand my skill set and build strong cross-functional abilities.
That’s why I joined HNG. At the time, I had only basic knowledge of marketing and quality assurance, but I wanted to fast-track my learning and push myself to acquire new skills quickly. HNG delivered exactly that, with well-crafted tasks across the tracks I interned in Sales & Marketing and Quality Assurance Engineering.
Now, having completed the program, I feel very confident. For example, I recently built a full go-to-market plan for the startup I currently work with. I also write test cases with clarity, understand what I’m documenting, and know the areas to check for defects. I can set up endpoint testing, and with a bit more training, I’m confident I’ll be able to handle automated testing as well.
Overall, my HNG experience is a perfect ten out of ten. It gave me practical skills, confidence, and the ability to deliver results in real-world product management.
converters.differently: If I were to do this again, what I would do differently is start my tasks earlier. It’s not that I didn’t begin on time, but balancing two tasks alongside a demanding full-time nine-to-five job consumed a lot of my time and made things more challenging. By starting earlier, I would feel less pressure and, as a result, be able to deliver even stronger outputs.
converters.bestTask: Stage 6: Enterprise B2B Growth Architecture Challenge
converters.worstTask: Stage 8a: Email Marketing Automation & Funnel Campaign
converters.takeaway:
L One of my biggest takeaways came from working extensively on Menucard. I was involved in many aspects of the product, drawing from both my own contributions and the strategies set by others, including founder-led initiatives and content calendars. A particularly eye-opening experience was simulating a live PR crisis and developing a brand recovery strategy. That exercise challenged my thinking in ways I had never considered before.
I also learned that a product’s longevity depends heavily on building the right community. Communities can take different forms, but the key is choosing the one that best aligns with the product itself. Additionally, working on Menucard’s website taught me that design is not just about aesthetics — it serves a clear purpose. From SEO copywriting to site structure, I saw how a well-built website can generate traffic, attract users, and drive leads. I applied both my QA and PM skills here, ensuring proper internal linking and compliance with WCAG design requirements.
Beyond Menucard, I also worked on FlareTag, where I contributed to rebranding and restructuring efforts. These experiences collectively strengthened my perspective as a Sales and Marketing intern, showing me how product management, marketing, and quality assurance intersect to create lasting impact.
converters.mentorsShout: Nwa, Mikey, Zikky, Jemi. Thank you dearest awesome mentors
converters.grateful: Bint Yahya, Mebud, Ana, Grace, all Socail badge Interns, all Sales and Marketing mentors, Jemi, Pomegranate.
converters.next: Application of all I’ve learnt into real world products and services to
converters.advice: Just be ready to research, read, write, edit and edit and more research.

converters.name: Kamba Juliet
converters.differently: I would socialize more, and I would be more rigorous with my tasks.
converters.bestTask: Stage 2: Boring Product to $10K Marketing Campaign
converters.worstTask: Stage 8a: Email Marketing Automation & Funnel Campaign
converters.takeaway: My biggest takeaway is resilience and persistence in product development. While working on Energy IQ, there were multiple challenges and moments where errors were identified or doubts were raised about the product. Despite this, we continued working and improving instead of stopping. This experience showed me that building products, especially in early-stage environments like startups, requires thick skin, adaptability, and a willingness to keep refining the work even when feedback is critical or progress feels slow.
converters.mentorsShout: You guys are amazing, thank you for everything.
converters.advice: Take every task as seriously as you can, be rigorous with it. Start your tasks early and do not wait till the dying minutes. Your social relations would matter more than you would ever imagine. Put yourself out there, make friends, network.

converters.name: Sosanwo Grace
converters.experience: It was a wonderful experience, it was stressful though but wonderful nonetheless
converters.differently: Nothing because changing something in the past my affect the future
converters.worstTask: Stage 1: Nigerian Startup Brand Audit & Analysis
converters.takeaway: My biggest takeaway is that marketing is not a support function. It is a product function. I came into HNG14 as a PM who was not sure she could do marketing. Working on Social Badge and the other task products taught me that the two disciplines are deeply connected. When you understand the product deeply, the marketing becomes more precise. When you understand the customer deeply, the product becomes more useful. Across every stage, from writing a brand strategy to building an email funnel to conducting keyword research and handling a PR crisis, I kept coming back to the same truth: clarity about who you are building for changes everything. I did not just learn marketing tools. I learned how to think like a marketer while still thinking like a product person. That combination is what I am taking forward.
converters.mentorsShout:
I want to give a genuine shout-out to every mentor who showed up for me during this internship. The feedback after every task did not just tell me what I got wrong, it told me why, and that distinction matters more than most people realise.
Nwa especially was someone who listened, really listened, stayed attentive, and showed up consistently. That kind of mentorship is not something you find everywhere and I do not take it for granted.
converters.next: QA is in view but I am working towards landing a Product Marketing Manager role as a junior associate.
converters.advice: Go prepared to learn and grow in a very short time.

converters.name: Ana Inyang
converters.experience: Apart from the fact that it reminded me of my Agency days, it also stretched me to learn Vibe Coding, and pushed me to make my first post on Linkedin and Substack (which I don't regret, could be the start of something great). But overall, I got to learn from other people, I got to experience collaborative competition, there were times I was frustrated and wanted to give up but it became evident that I had built grit, perseverance and resilience.
converters.differently: Huddle with other interns to find out how they
converters.bestTask: Stage 8a: Email Marketing Automation & Funnel Campaign
converters.worstTask: Stage 4a: CV/Profile Card for Team Matching
converters.takeaway: From all the task products I worked on, it gave me insights into the strategic outlook of the brands, what they did that brought them success. It allowed me to see the various dynamics of audience behaviour across different territories and how a brand can stay relevant across various markets. Most importantly, of a product is not working and demand isn't generated find out what is wrong, make changes especially within the messaging and set clear KPI's to measure success.
converters.mentorsShout:

I'm still waiting for the money he seemily promised to send us during award night because his daily limit has been long renewed. You get to understand that he was the KingPin in the Arena to ensure that the best of the bests emerged as finalist.

S/O to Nwa, aka Pep, aka El Capitan, thank you for being the voice of reasoning, charm and seeker of collaboration, you always looked out for us, reminded us, encouraged us, helped us. You literally wanted to see us succeed, and you kept pushing some of us to believe in ourselves.

S/O to CHIEF ZIKKY, This Hall of fame task you dropped is missing a section, i'll talk about that later. But you had your way of showing up in the most unexpected way and nudging us to keep going and to believe in ourself.

S/O to Fifi and Tall Girl, you both were the birds that kept singing praises even after the melody stopped. Thank you for you do.

P.s I feel like this Form is missing a field where we get to input our account number. We don too try abeg. Because I don't like stressing my mentors, i've made it easy, my number is on my profile, just remove the first Zero and you have my opay.. Gracias.. LoL
converters.grateful: Mebud, BK (Bree), BintYahya, Osajimere (Anvila), Menim, Grace finished in 2 Tracks in i14
converters.advice: You think anybody can do marketing right, this is not a walk in a park, this is an arena that seperates kings from wannabes. If you believe you have the skin for to power growth within products then take a shot, othewise look for an easier track.

converters.name: Sophia St-Matthew
converters.experience: My experience was an interesting one. It was challenging and it pushed me to be better and do better and I learnt way more that I thought I would.
converters.differently: I'd make more friends, ask questions more and be less introverted.
converters.bestTask: Stage 5a: Crisis PR & Brand Recovery Strategy
converters.takeaway: Working on SpecFlow taught me that even highly technical products need simple, compelling positioning no matter how technical we think the users are so they can understand their value better. Overall, I learned that successful marketing ie basically understanding people, solving problems, and communicating value in a way that make people take action.
converters.mentorsShout: Shout out to El Capitán for always being so nice and fun and bubbly (pls never change) and caring about us.
converters.grateful: I'm grateful for BintYahya. Thank you for being so nice to me and always asking me hiw my tasks were going even when I kept dying in silence. And also going through your tasks submission also helped be better and documentation and presentation my works and also research. Mebud and Menim too, going through his submission were always really good for me, because it helped me make better submissions.
converters.favProduct: I loved working on SkillBridge/CredLane and GreyPalNG. And lowkey SpecFlow
converters.next: After HNG, I'm going to sleep for like 3 days then, maybe start job hunting and maybe come back next cohort because what is my life without a little chaos and stress😭. But I need to sleep first🫠
converters.advice: I'd tell them to first make friends, from their cohorts and different cohorts. Ask questions, as many questions that comes to their heads. If you're not top 5, go through their submissions, even if you are, still do it. It'll help you understand marketing from different perspectives and help you grow in the cohort. Read articles and watch YouTube videos around marketing, it really helps too.

converters.name: Sochima Mmadueke
converters.experience: Through the program, it was both challenging and rewarding. It pushed me out of my comfort zone and helped me develop practical skills in sales, marketing, research, content creation, and teamwork. Working on different tasks and products gave me a better understanding of how ideas are transformed into solutions that create value for users. I also had the opportunity to learn from mentors and collaborate with talented interns, which improved my communication, problem-solving, and professional skills. Overall, the experience was a significant step in my personal and career growth, and it has increased my confidence in pursuing opportunities in the tech and marketing space.
converters.differently: If I had the opportunity to do it again, I would be more proactive from the beginning. I would ask more questions, seek feedback earlier, and take advantage of more networking opportunities with mentors and fellow interns. While I learned a lot throughout the internship, starting with this mindset earlier would have helped me maximize my learning and contributions even more
converters.bestTask: Stage 5a: Crisis PR & Brand Recovery Strategy
converters.worstTask: Stage 8b: Website Build, SEO & Conversion Optimization
converters.takeaway: My biggest takeaway from all the products I worked on is that successful products are built around real user problems, not assumptions. Throughout the internship, I learned the value of conducting research, gathering feedback, and adapting strategies based on user needs. I also discovered how important collaboration is, as product success depends on effective teamwork between marketing, product, and other stakeholders. This experience strengthened my ability to think from the customer's perspective and make decisions that drive both user satisfaction and business growth.
converters.mentorsShout: A special shout-out to all the SM mentors for making this internship such a valuable experience. Thank you for your guidance, constructive feedback, and constant encouragement. One of the things I appreciated most was how approachable and supportive most of you were, always willing to listen, guide, and push us to do better. Your dedication to helping us grow made a lasting impact. Thank you 🙏❤️ you guys rock 🌹
converters.favProduct: I loved working with lazy-panda🐼, the concept at which the product is built on is real and is something I myself struggle with so I enjoyed working on it.
converters.next: The big picture now getting an opportunity with a local or international organization or company.
converters.advice: To future sales and marketing interns, HNG will break and remold you in so many ways. Come prepared and be open-minded from the beginning, don't be shy to ask questions, and take every piece of feedback seriously. Must important make friends from day 1.
